reasoning: Jurassic World Rebirth faces substantial headwinds to becoming the 2025 domestic box office champion. Crucially, recent reports (May 2025) indicate it will suffer a significant loss of lucrative IMAX screens to competitors like "F1" (releasing June 27) and "Superman" (mid-July), strictly capping its gross potential, as premium large formats are crucial for VFX blockbusters. Historically, the franchise has been trending downward domestically (Jurassic World $653M -> Fallen Kingdom $417M -> Dominion $376M). It is competing in a crowded summer block, and has to outperform early-year heavyweights like 'A Minecraft Movie' (over $280M expected domestic by mid-April alone) and end-of-year juggernauts like 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' (which, even with a December 19 release, could easily replicate Avatar 2's $400M+ calendar-year domestic pull). The Polymarket consensus of 5% from late May accurately encapsulates its underdog status, and the confirmation of it losing IMAX screens justifies shifting the probability slightly lower to 4%.
